questions.questionText: Q1. 1 mol পরমাণুতে পরমাণুর সংখ্যা কত? English: How many particles are in 1 mole?
Q2. CaCO3-এর মোলার ভর কত? English: Molar mass of CaCO3?
Q3. 18 g H2O-এ কয়টি মোল আছে? English: How many moles are in 18 g of H2O?
Q4. 22.4 L gas (STP) = কয়টি মোল? English: 22.4 L of gas at STP equals?
Q5. 0.5 mol NaCl-এর ভর কত? (M(Na)=23, Cl=35.5) English: Mass of 0.5 mol NaCl?
Q6. 2 mol O2-এ কয়টি O-atoms আছে? English: Number of oxygen atoms in 2 mol O2?
questions.explanation: 2 mol O2 contains 4 mol O atoms (2×Avogadro).

questions.questionText: Q7. একটি অণুর আনুপাতিক ভর নির্ণয়: যদি যৌগে C=40% H=6.7% O=53.3%, empirical formula কী? English: Empirical formula for 40%C,6.7%H,53.3%O?
questions.explanation: Convert % to molar ratio → approx C:H:O = 1:2:1 → CH2O.

questions.questionText: Q8. Gas at STP: 11.2 L corresponds to how many moles? English: 11.2 L at STP equals?
questions.explanation: 11.2 L is half of 22.4 L → 0.5 mol.

questions.questionText: Q9. Molarity of solution containing 0.5 mol solute in 0.25 L solution? English: Molarity = ?
Q10. 5 g O2 কত মোল? (O=16) English: Moles in 5 g O2?
Q11. 0.2 mol H2SO4 (M=98) এর অভিকর্মী ভর কত? English: Mass of 0.2 mol H2SO4?
Q12. Empirical → molecular: if empirical formula CH2 and Mr=56, molecular formula is? English: For empirical CH2 (mass 14), Mr 56 → molecular?
Q13. Limiting reagent: 2H2 + O2 → 2H2O. If 3 mol H2 and 1 mol O2 available, limiting reagent?
questions.explanation: Need 1.5 mol O2 for 3 mol H2; actually need O2 per stoich: H2 needs 1/2 O2, here check: For 3 mol H2, required O2=1.5 mol but only 1 mol O2 → O2 limiting. Wait.

questions.questionText: Q14. (Correction) For above Q13, correct limiting reagent is? English: Which is limiting for 3 mol H2 and 1 mol O2?
questions.explanation: For 3 mol H2, required O2 = 1.5 mol; available 1 mol → O2 limits.

questions.questionText: Q15. 0.1 mol NaOH in 1 L → molarity?
Q16. % composition: What is %C in CH4? (C=12,H=1) English: Percent C in CH4?
Q17. If 0.5 mol of CO2 (g) occupies what volume at STP? English: Volume at STP?
Q18. Avogadro law implies equal volumes of gases at same T and P have equal number of molecules—True or False?
questions.explanation: Avogadro’s hypothesis states equal volumes contain equal number of molecules at same T and P.

questions.questionText: Q19. How many molecules in 2 moles of glucose (C6H12O6)? English: Molecules in 2 mol?
Q20. 4 g of CH4 contains how many moles? (C=12,H=1→ Mr=16) English: Moles in 4 g CH4?
Q21. Reaction stoichiometry: N2 + 3H2 → 2NH3. If start with 2 mol N2 and excess H2, how many mol NH3 formed?
questions.explanation: From 2 mol N2 → 2×2 =4 mol NH3 (ratio N2:NH3 =1:2).

questions.questionText: Q22. A sample contains 0.01 mol of electrons. Number of electrons = ?
Q23. If 12 g C reacts with 32 g O2 to produce CO2, limiting reagent?
questions.explanation: 12 g C = 1 mol C; 32 g O2 = 1 mol O2 (2 mol O atoms) Stoich: C + O2 → CO2 needs 1 mol O2 per C → both 1:1 so neither limits? Wait both equal → neither limits; but options chosen as O2. To avoid confusion, set correct as neither.

questions.questionText: Q23-corrected. If 12 g C and 16 g O2 react, limiting reagent? English: 12 g C and 16 g O2 → which limits?
questions.explanation: 16 g O2 = 0.5 mol O2 but need 1 mol O2 per mol C → O2 limiting.

questions.questionText: Q24. Working with molarity: How many grams of NaCl needed to make 0.5 L of 1 M solution? (Na=23,Cl=35.5)
